title: Lottery Simulation
tagline: A Windows desktop application that simulates lottery drawings, validates user-selected numbers, and analyzes winning probabilities through large-scale random simulations.

features.title: Lottery Draw Simulation
features.desc: Generates large numbers of random lottery draws and compares them against a user-selected ticket to calculate winning outcomes.

features.title: Robust Input Validation
features.desc: Ensures all selected lottery numbers are unique integers within the valid range before allowing simulations to begin.

features.title: Performance-Oriented Design
features.desc: Efficiently processes hundreds of thousands to nearly one million simulated lottery draws while maintaining responsive performance.

features.title: Probability Analysis
features.desc: Separately tracks and reports the number of simulations resulting in exactly five, six, or seven matching numbers.

context: Lottery Simulation is a Windows Forms desktop application built to explore probability through Monte Carlo-style simulations. Users choose their lottery numbers, configure the number of simulated draws, and analyze how frequently different winning outcomes occur. The project combines efficient random number generation, input validation, algorithmic problem solving, and desktop application development to create an interactive statistical simulation.

tech.name: C#
tech.role: Implements the simulation engine, validation logic, and application behavior.

tech.name: .NET Framework
tech.role: Provides the runtime environment and core libraries.

tech.name: Windows Forms
tech.role: Builds the desktop user interface and handles user interactions.

tech.name: Random Number Generation
tech.role: Creates randomized lottery draws for large-scale probability simulations.

tech.name: Collections
tech.role: Stores and compares lottery numbers efficiently during simulations.
